title: Food Stabilization Flow
description: End-to-end flow for stabilizing the household food situation: assess storehouse, plan meals, align garden/animals, preserve surplus, and lock in stable daily routines.
objectives: Ensure a minimum horizon of stable meals based on current inventory.
Align garden and animal outputs with storehouse gaps and goals.
Capture and preserve any surplus before spoilage.
Establish routine cooking and cleaning sessions that keep the system stable.

entryCriteria.description: When to consider activating this flow.

exitCriteria.description: When the household food situation is considered 'stable' for now.

nowCtaBehavior.description: How the 'Now' button should behave when this flow is active.

assessment.label: Assessment & Horizon Check
assessment.goal: Understand how many days of stable meals you can support and where the gaps are.

steps.label: Run Inventory Audit
steps.description: Take a quick snapshot of current pantry, fridge, and freezer to understand what you actually have.
steps.nowCtaHint: If no recent audit exists, suggest this first.

steps.label: Update Household Nutrition Targets
steps.description: Make sure your nutrition or health calculators reflect your current household goals.
steps.nowCtaHint: Optional but recommended if health goals recently changed.

steps.label: Check Meal Horizon
steps.description: Estimate how many days of balanced meals you can support based on current inventory and targets.
steps.nowCtaHint: Use output to decide if you need emergency stabilization or normal planning.

storehouse_stabilization.label: Storehouse Stabilization
storehouse_stabilization.goal: Close immediate gaps, reduce waste risk, and build a realistic meal plan from what you have.

steps.label: Resolve Critical Gaps
steps.description: Identify critical shortages (staples, protein, fats) and decide whether to buy, substitute, or re-plan.
steps.nowCtaHint: If critical gaps exist, prioritize this before detailed meal planning.

steps.label: Create Meal Plan from Storehouse
steps.description: Generate a 7–14 day meal plan anchored in what is actually in your storehouse.
steps.nowCtaHint: Primary planning step; strongly recommended every stabilization run.

steps.label: Generate Shopping/Restock List
steps.description: Create a targeted shopping list to close remaining gaps and avoid random impulse purchases.
steps.nowCtaHint: Only if outside purchases are part of your stabilization strategy.

production_alignment.label: Garden & Animals Alignment
production_alignment.goal: Aim your garden and animal outputs at storehouse gaps, not random surplus.

steps.label: Align Garden Plan with Storehouse Gaps
steps.description: Plan what to plant and when based on medium-term shortages and preferred meals.
steps.nowCtaHint: Use after meal/storehouse gaps have been identified.

steps.label: Align Animal Plan with Protein/Fat Needs
steps.description: Plan breeding, culling, or butchery windows so your storehouse has stable protein and fat.
steps.nowCtaHint: Especially important if you rely heavily on your own meat/eggs/dairy.

steps.label: Prepare Harvest & Butchery Logging
steps.description: Ensure harvest and butchery logs are configured so future events automatically feed storehouse and preservation suggestions.
steps.nowCtaHint: One-time or occasional configuration step; boosts automation quality.

preservation_lock_in.label: Preservation Lock-In
preservation_lock_in.goal: Capture surplus from garden, animals, or batch cooking and convert it into stable, long-term storehouse value.

steps.label: Identify Surplus and Urgent Items
steps.description: Scan storehouse, harvest logs, and butchery yields for items at high risk of spoilage.
steps.nowCtaHint: If spoilage risk is high, this step should override normal cooking sessions.

steps.label: Plan Preservation Session
steps.description: Map surplus into canning, freezing, dehydrating, or curing sessions.
steps.nowCtaHint: Creates a concrete checklist that can be run in the SessionRunner.

steps.label: Run Preservation Session
steps.description: Execute preservation tasks with a guided session, timers, and safety cues.
steps.nowCtaHint: This should be a top candidate for 'Now' when spoilage risk is high.

steps.label: Update Storehouse with Preserved Items
steps.description: Add jars, frozen packs, and dried goods to storehouse inventory and savings.
steps.nowCtaHint: Can be prompted immediately after preservation session completes.

routine_loops.label: Routine Cooking & Cleaning Loops
routine_loops.goal: Turn the plan into daily reality and keep the kitchen environment stable and pleasant.

steps.label: Run Batch Cooking Session
steps.description: Execute planned meals in a batch session to reduce daily workload and increase predictability.
steps.nowCtaHint: Prime candidate for daily/weekly 'Now' button when a meal plan exists.

steps.label: Run Kitchen Reset Session
steps.description: Clean and reset kitchen zones after cooking or preservation to keep the environment ready for the next session.
steps.nowCtaHint: Often suggested immediately after cooking/preservation sessions end.

steps.label: Adjust Storehouse after Sessions
steps.description: Update inventory for ingredients used and leftovers produced from cooking sessions.
steps.nowCtaHint: Can be a lightweight panel in SessionRunner's 'done' state.
